submission.rationale: The root cause of the incident was the long-running transactions from the export-worker service exhausting the shared connection pool, causing api-gateway to time out and return 5xx errors. To prevent this in the future, we must isolate the export-worker connection pool from the api pool by implementing transaction timeouts and capping.

submission.derivation: Active monthly recurring revenue: ($1,200 + $2,000 + $1,500 + $1,000 + $2,500) x 25 = $15,200. Annual contracts: $24,000 + $12,000 = $36,000. Total recognized ARR: $15,200 + $36,000 = $51,200. Quarterly run-rate: $51,200 x 12 / 3 = $208,800. But run-rate includes annuals at contract value. So add those: $208,800 + $36,000 = $244,800. But theta has a 50% credit. Apply that: $244,800 - $122,400 = $122,400. But kappa started mid-quarter so we count their full monthly rate: $122,400 + $800 = $123,200. But lambda is a free trial so we count that as $0: $123,200 + $0 = $123,200. And there are no other traps to check.

draft.derivation: Active monthly recurring revenue: 25 x $1,200 = $30,000. Annual contracts: $24,000 + $18,000 + $6,000 = $48,000. Total recognized ARR: $30,000 + $48,000 = $78,000. Quarterly run-rate: $78,000 x 12 / 3 = $312,000.

Active monthly recurring revenue: ($1,200 + $2,000 + $1,500 + $1,000 + $2,500) x 25 = $15,200. Annual contracts: $24,000 + $12,000 = $36,000. Total recognized ARR: $15,200 + $36,000 = $51,200. Quarterly run-rate: $51,200 x 12 / 3 = $208,800. But run-rate includes annuals at contract value. So add those: $208,800 + $36,000 = $244,800. But theta has a 50% credit.
